ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for chicken kebab pizza; FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) used as the closest broader class. Chicken kebab topping variant is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) used as the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for Indian-style chicken kebab pizza in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken kebab pizza as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one slice of chicken kebab pizza weighing approximately 100 g, representing roughly one-eighth of a standard thin-crust pizza.

nutrient_source.title: Kebab Pizza - Chicken (HPB Singapore Food Composition Database)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Kebab Pizza - Chicken (cr_id G00010655). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central pizza composition data (USDA SR Legacy #21299) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — thin-crust pizza with cheese and meat toppings (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken kebab pizza. GI of 60 is estimated from thin-crust pizza with meat and cheese toppings as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Thin-crust pizza with protein and fat toppings typically falls in the medium GI range (55–65). Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (60 × 5.24) / 100 = 3.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeding the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit or avoid this dish; a single 100 g serving delivers 1080 mg sodium, representing approximately 47% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit, and regular consumption will significantly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (5.24 g/100 g) with high protein and fat slowing gastric emptying and blunting postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate load (5.24 g/100 g, GI ~60, GL ~3.1 per 100 g) makes this dish relatively glycaemia-friendly for people with Type 2 Diabetes, though the very high sodium content (1080 mg/100 g) remains a concurrent cardiovascular risk that requires monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g), and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: People with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish only occasionally and in small portions; the combination of high saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g), high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) is strongly associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and arterial blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g) exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; high sodium and nightshade vegetables may exacerbate joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this dish due to high saturated fat and sodium content; bell peppers, tomato, and tomato sauce are nightshades that may trigger symptom flares in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Kebab Pizza is a thin-crust pizza of Indian-fusion origin, popular in Singapore's multicultural food scene. It features a thin wheat-flour base topped with kebab-marinated chicken pieces, mozzarella cheese, tomato sauce, red onion, fresh tomato, and capsicum (bell pepper). The dish is baked in a pizza oven, yielding a crispy base with a savoury, spiced topping that blends South Asian kebab flavours with Italian pizza format. The texture is crisp at the crust edges, soft and cheesy at the centre, with tender spiced chicken throughout. Nutritionally, it is high in total fat (26.4 g/100 g) and saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g) from mozzarella and cooking fat, very high in sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and relatively low in carbohydrates (5.24 g/100 g) due to the thin crust and protein-dominant topping profile. Protein is moderate at 10.7 g/100 g. The low carbohydrate content gives it a moderate glycaemic index (~60) and a low glycaemic load (~3.1 per 100 g serving), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management. However, the very high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant portion restriction. It is classified as a non-vegetarian, halal-friendly dish and is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner. The dish is listed in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id G00010655) with a confidence of 0.95.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in kebab pizza?
Can diabetics eat chicken kebab pizza?
Is kebab pizza high in fat?
What is the glycemic index of chicken pizza?
Is kebab pizza suitable for hypertension?
Calories in chicken kebab pizza per 100g

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010655, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy pizza composition data as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; this single field is medium confidence. All other values are exact HPB matches.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #21299 (Pizza with meat and vegetables, thin crust) as the HPB database does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. This estimate is consistent with the total fat of 26.4 g/100 g and the known mozzarella and chicken fat composition. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this dish, and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to supply these values without unacceptable estimation uncertainty. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; the thin crust and processed toppings contribute negligible fibre, confirmed by the HPB source. 4. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on thin-crust pizza with meat and cheese toppings from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement exists for chicken kebab pizza. 5. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style 'Kebab Pizza- Chicken' to the canonical form 'Chicken Kebab Pizza' for database consistency. 6. Halal status is inferred from the Indian-Muslim culinary context and the HPB listing; formal halal certification status should be verified with the restaurant operator.
